creator: apeiron+freebsd-bugs@isuckatdomains.net
text:
On Sun, Apr 15, 2012 at 13:15:32 +0000 , scheidell@FreeBSD.org wrote:
> Synopsis: net-mgmt/nagios does not need PHP
> 
> State-Changed-From-To: feedback->closed
> State-Changed-By: scheidell
> State-Changed-When: Sun Apr 15 13:15:32 UTC 2012
> State-Changed-Why: 
> Feedback Received. Maintainer recommends against change.
> 
> http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=166807

Sorry for the late reply on this.

Would it be possible to make the web frontend optional? I will not be
using it in my configuration and would like the option of not requiring
it. nagios builds happily without PHP installed (nor even libgd for that
matter) which suggests that the frontend features are not required.

If this is acceptable, I'm fine providing a patch. Or, alternately,
would a child port be a better idea?

-- 
Thanks and best regards,
Chris Nehren
